consumer-cards.notes: USA is the world's #1 card market. ~480 card transactions per capita per year (2.7x EU average). Card spend exceeded $10T for the first time in 2024. Merchant processing fees $187.2B. Credit cards = 35%, debit = 30% of consumer payments (Federal Reserve Payments Study). 153.3B GP card transactions in 2022 (latest Fed data).

sources.citation: Payments Dive / Nilson — US card market share 20.6%
GlobeNewsWire / Nilson — US card spend exceeded $10T

bank-transfers.notes: ACH 35.2B transactions in 2025 ($93T value). Including off-network: 42.1B. Same Day ACH: 1.4B (+16.7% YoY). B2B ACH: 8.1B (+9.9%). Fedwire: 209.9M txns ($1,133T). CHIPS: ~126M txns est. ($454T est.). FedNow: ~415K txns total in first 8 months. US is 7.3% of global bank transfers by count but dominant by value (Fedwire alone = $1,133T).

digital-wallets.notes: US digital wallets are predominantly card-rail overlays. Apple Pay ~20B transactions (2023 est.), Google Pay non-UPI US ~2-3B, Samsung Pay ~1B. Nearly all ride on Visa/Mastercard rails = double-counted with Consumer Cards. For de-duplication, only non-card-rail wallet transactions count (~2-3B incremental). Gross figure shown here for category comparison.

p2p-transfers.notes: USA dominates global P2P (>90% of dedicated P2P volume). Zelle: 3.6B txns, $1.04T (crossed $1T for first time in 2024). Venmo: ~2.3B txns, ~$280B. Cash App: ~1.5B txns, ~$283B. PayPal P2P: ~0.3B. UPI/WeChat P2P classified separately under Digital Wallets. Zelle avg transfer $289 vs UPI avg $17.

sources.citation: CNBC — Zelle Payments Top $1 Trillion in 2024

ecommerce.notes: US e-commerce ~$1.2T GMV in 2024. Amazon: ~4.5B orders/year. Shopify US merchants: ~1B+ (est.). US is #2 e-commerce market after China. E-commerce ~16% of US retail. Overlay with card payments (most e-commerce paid by card).

sources.citation: Capital One Shopping — Amazon orders per day

bnpl.notes: US BNPL: Affirm ~0.2B purchases ($28B GMV), Afterpay ~0.4B ($25B), Klarna US portion, PayPal Pay Later US portion. US is ~28% of global BNPL purchases by count. Each purchase generates ~3.6 installment events. Overlap with card/bank rails is near-complete.

bill-payments.notes: ~150M utility accounts x 12 = ~1.8B utility payments. ~350M telecom subscriptions x 12 = ~4.2B. ~200M+ digital subscriptions x 12 = ~2.4B. Plus rent, insurance premiums (separate capsule), and other recurring. Most flow through ACH direct debit or card-on-file. ~90%+ overlay with existing payment rails.

payroll.notes: ~150M formal workers. 70% biweekly (26/yr), 20% weekly (52/yr), 10% monthly (12/yr). Total ~3.4B payroll transactions. ADP covers 26M+ employees (1 in 6 private sector). ~90%+ flows through ACH. US biweekly cycle generates 2.2x more transactions per worker than monthly-pay countries.

equity-markets.notes: US equity ADV: 12.2B shares, ~37.7M trades/day est. Annual: ~9.5B trades. NYSE + NASDAQ combined market cap >$50T (world's two largest by cap). Off-exchange: 47% of TCV (record high). Options: 47.3M contracts ADV. ETF: 19.6% of equity volume. Retail: 17.9%. Average trade size ~$1,730 (vs India ~$500).

etd.notes: CME ADV 26.5M (record, +9%). Cboe options 3.8B contracts (record 5th yr). ICE total 2B contracts (1.2B commodity). Combined US ETD ~13B contracts. US is 6.3% by contract count (India 62%) but ~45-50% by notional value. CME dominates interest rate derivatives (13.7M ADV) and sets global rate benchmarks.

commodities.notes: CME commodity segments ~3.5B contracts (energy, metals, agriculture all record in 2024). ICE commodity: 1.2B (oil 655M, environmental 20.4M). US dominates global commodity price discovery. Brent crude benchmark = 75% of internationally traded oil. Overlap with ETD category (commodities are a subset of ETD).

key_systems: NY Fed FX Committee dealer banks
forex.notes: USD is one side of 88% of all FX trades (BIS 2025). Global FX: $9.6T/day. US-attributed ~40% based on NY Fed FX Committee data. CME FX futures ADV 1.0-1.2M contracts (record). CLS settles $7.2T/day across 18 currencies. Attribution complex — USD involvement != US execution.

fixed-income.notes: Treasury ADV $1,055B (first time >$1T). Corporate bond ADV $57.9B (+11.5%). TRACE: ~52M bond trades/year. MSRB: 14.5M muni trades (record, 17.6M in 2025 +22%). Treasury: ~12.6M trades est. US = ~70% of global cash bond trade count. $36T+ Treasuries outstanding. Global outstanding $145T.

sources.citation: MSRB — 2024 Municipal Market Year in Review

otc-derivatives.notes: US hosts 4 of 5 largest OTC derivative dealer banks. ISDA SwapsInfo Q4 2024 US-only: 696.6K IRD trades (+14.1%). US share of ISDA-reported IRD: ~40-45%. CDS index + single-name: significant US share. Total excl. FX: ~1.9M US trades/year. Low-frequency but massive notional ($732T global outstanding).

interbank-rtgs.notes: Fedwire: 209.9M transactions, $1,133T value in 2024. Daily avg: 836,322 txns, $4.5T. Avg transfer $5.4M. Fedwire = single highest-value payment system on Earth. US is ~50% of global RTGS by transaction count (210M of 423M). By value, Fedwire alone is ~53% of the 5 major RTGS systems combined.

cex.notes: Coinbase: ~$1T+ trading volume (2024), largest US exchange, publicly traded. Kraken: $665B volume. Combined US ~$1.7T. US share ~4.9% of global $77.3T (adjusted). CME Bitcoin futures ADV +56% to 19K contracts. USDC (Circle, US-regulated) = 70% of stablecoin transfer volume. Post-regulation clarity attracting institutional capital.

government-payments.notes: IRS e-filed returns (FY2024): 219.9M (all forms), 147.1M individual e-filed. E-file rate 96% (2025 season). Social Security: 71.6M beneficiaries, ~864M annual disbursements. SSI: 7.4M recipients. Medicare/Medicaid: ~1.5B claims. Federal/military payroll: ~3.4M workers, ~86M paychecks/yr. State benefits (unemployment, SNAP): ~2B+ est.

atm-withdrawals.notes: ~470K ATMs (most of any country). ~7.5B withdrawals in 2024 (-6% YoY). Avg withdrawal $198 (among highest globally). Declining as card/digital payments substitute. Zero overlap with electronic payments — ATM transactions are cash-out events.

de_duplicated_usa_tps.methodology: Sum of de-duplicated contributions: Consumer Cards (5,040 — primary payment rail, excludes overlay wallets) + Bank Transfers/ACH (1,116 — non-card bank transfers; Fedwire/CHIPS counted under RTGS) + P2P Transfers (244 — Zelle/Venmo/CashApp, partially on bank rails but distinct economic transactions) + E-Commerce (incremental ~50 — 90%+ already on card/bank rails) + BNPL (incremental ~10 — near-complete overlay) + Bill Payments (incremental ~150 — portion not on card/ACH) + Payroll (incremental ~30 — portion not already in ACH) + Equity Markets (540) + ETD (600) + Commodities (overlap with ETD, not double-counted) + Forex (16) + Fixed Income (74) + OTC Derivatives (2) + Interbank RTGS (7) + CEX (149) + Government Payments (incremental ~100 — portion not on existing rails) + ATM Withdrawals (238 — zero overlap). Adjusted for card-wallet overlap, bill-payment rail overlap, payroll-ACH overlap.
de_duplicated_usa_tps.notes: Range reflects uncertainty in card-wallet de-duplication (Apple Pay overlay), bill payment rail attribution, government payment channel mix, and FX/OTC attribution methodology. Reserve currency effect adds ~3,000-4,000 TPS to US attribution across FX, fixed income, and derivatives.

confidence.notes: Strong data quality from regulated US institutions: Nacha (ACH), FRB Services (Fedwire/FedNow), SIFMA (bonds), FINRA TRACE, MSRB, CME Group, Cboe, IRS, SSA. Card data anchored by Nilson Report and Federal Reserve Payments Study. Weakest areas: digital wallet transaction counts (Apple Pay doesn't disclose), FX and OTC attribution (USD involvement != US execution), government payment disaggregation. P2P data strong from Zelle (public company), weaker for Venmo/Cash App (estimated from parent company filings).
